title: rosa multiflora var. brachyacantha
intro: Rosa multiflora var. brachyacantha is a variety of the multiflora rose, a thorny, deciduous shrub native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa multiflora var. brachyacantha include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, as well as its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
